slides.title: Executive summary
slides.body:
**Executive Comparison:**  
The Access Review Evidence Log (EVD-101) is a formal inventory tracking evidence status for the Q1 2024 Northwind vendor-control review, where missing or incomplete items—such as the archived email-export artifact for EV-002—must be resolved before control closure. In contrast, the Privileged Account Approval Email grants standing access to two named administrators under a pilot program, contingent on strict scoping, logging, and quarterly revalidation, with access contingent on ongoing compliance rather than evidence completion.

- The Access Review Evidence Log requires resolution of missing evidence items before control closure; the Privileged Account Email does not require evidence completion but mandates quarterly recertification.  
- EV-002’s compliance depends on archiving an email-export artifact in an approved repository; the Privileged Account Email requires vendor confirmation and revalidation upon personnel changes.  
- The Access Review Evidence Log is tied to a specific vendor-control review cycle (Q1 2024); the Privileged Account Email supports ongoing, standing access under a pilot framework.  
- Missing items in the Access Review Evidence Log block control closure; access in the Privileged Account Email is conditional on adherence to scoping and logging rules, not evidence completeness.  
- The Access Review Evidence Log tracks repository locations and status of evidence items; the Privileged Account Email does not reference repository tracking but emphasizes audit-ready logging and governance.  
- Control closure for the Access Review Evidence Log is contingent on provenance verification of all items; the Privileged Account Email requires continuous oversight and revalidation, not a one-time evidence resolution.
